hero.name: Leonardo Spolador
hero.headline: Designing for the people who run complex energy systems
hero.subheadline: Battery analytics, wind operations, grid-connected platforms. Seventeen years of design practice, the last several where a wrong call has real consequences.

outcomes.label: Revenue growth linked to product design decisions
People trained across design and product CoPs
Reduction in time-to-value for critical workflows

positioning: My work has been in energy systems for the past several years: predictive battery analytics, wind farm operations, grid-connected platforms. Environments where software is the infrastructure people depend on to make real decisions. Where a missed risk signal, a wrong maintenance call, or an opaque dataset has consequences in the physical world.
That context shapes how I think about design — not as screens, but as a system for turning complexity into clarity.

Building the right conditions for that work matters as much as doing it. At ExxonMobil, that meant building a Design Community of Practice that reached 100 people across product, engineering, and PM — and kept running after I left. I co-founded two companies early in my career, and what I learned is that a team is itself a system. One that gets stronger when people have room to grow, experiment, and find their best contribution. My way of leading is side by side: close to the craft, working with the team, keeping direction clear enough that people can move fast with confidence.

case_studies.title: From scattered modules to a platform operators depend on daily
case_studies.description: Shaped product strategy and earned design influence without authority — from a single State of Health chart to TWAICE's flagship product.

case_studies.title: The program I built to make myself unnecessary
case_studies.description: Grew design capability across 100+ people in a global organisation. The program kept running after I left.

case_studies.title: Designing for trust in a market where trust is the product
case_studies.description: Research-led design under vendor resistance. Defended a mobile-first direction backed by market data — from discovery through usability validation.

case_studies.title: Designing operational clarity for the people who run wind farms
case_studies.description: Unifying fragmented legacy workflows into a coherent operational platform. Case study in progress.

testimonial.quote: Leonardo has consistently demonstrated resilience and a highly collaborative spirit. His exceptional ability to clearly articulate design decisions facilitates smooth cross-functional collaboration and empowers less experienced designers.
testimonial.name: Oliver Eckstein
testimonial.title: Head of Product & UX, TWAICE

contact.heading: If the problem involves complex systems and people making real decisions under pressure — that's the conversation I'm built for.
contact.body: If you're building software for energy systems — platforms that operators, utilities, or consumers depend on to make real decisions — and thinking about what design leadership looks like at your stage, I'd like to talk.
